page: Amazon Web Services t3a.small is a burstable performance instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.2 GHz. The configuration provides 2 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) mapped to 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. It includes 2.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, offering 1.0 GB of RAM per core. The CPU cache configuration consists of 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 8 MB L3 cache. There is no local storage or dedicated GPU hardware bundled with this instance type, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 0 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the performance of the t3a.small in the lower tiers. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests both score around 590, placing the instance in the bottom 10% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth benchmarks indicate read-only speeds of 77.12 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 20.84 GB/sec for larger uncached blocks, while large block write speeds reach 12.35 GB/sec. The PassMark CPU score is 1862.31, and the PassMark memory score is 846.47. In application-specific tests, the instance achieves 1.26 million operations per second in Redis SET operations and a static web serving throughput of 1.59 GB/sec. Large language model inference using a small 135M model yields 41.1 tokens per second for prompt processing and 22.84 tokens per second for text generation.

The burstable CPU allocation model represents a significant tradeoff, as continuous high CPU utilization will deplete CPU credits and restrict performance. However, this architecture offers high qualitative cost efficiency for workloads with intermittent resource demands, allowing users to avoid paying for idle dedicated compute capacity. Given its hardware profile and benchmark results, the t3a.small is best suited for low-traffic web servers, small microservices, development and testing environments, code repositories, and basic background scripting tasks that do not require sustained high CPU or memory throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services t3a.small is a burstable performance instance designed for entry-level workloads. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.2 GHz, providing 2 vCPUs across 1 physical core, and 2.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance does not include local storage or a GPU, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates modest performance, with stress-ng and PassMark scores falling into the bottom 10% of tested instances. The burstable CPU allocation model offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads with variable resource demands. This instance is best suited for low-traffic web serving, development environments, and lightweight background tasks.

tagline: A burstable, entry-level x86_64 compute instance designed for lightweight workloads and development environments with variable resource demands.

Utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model via the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Delivers entry-level performance suitable for low-traffic web serving
No local storage or dedicated GPU resources included
Best suited for development environments, microservices, and lightweight background tasks
